key_insight: An agent can start from axioms and inference rules alone, then self-supervise its way to discovering and reusing useful theorems without relying on human theorem libraries.
engineering_relevance: This suggests a scalable path to formally verifiable mathematical knowledge bases that can boost automated proof search and improve LLM reasoning via promptable lemmas.
method: Alternate proof search with useful-theorem extraction.
Add discovered theorems to a growing reusable library.
Reuse library entries as lemmas in later searches to compound progress.
evidence_signal: In experiments, the system discovered tens of thousands of theorems and solved human-written benchmark problems, while the discovered theorems also improved LLM proof performance when used as prompt lemmas.
watch_item: Results depend on a formal axiomatic setting, so practical impact may vary with proof-system design, search cost, and how well discovered theorems transfer to new domains.
paper_title: Self-Supervised Theorem Discovery in a Formal Axiomatic System
